description_long: This object consists of two pole-mounted traffic lights, each with a rectangular yellow backplate and a single cylindrical signal light protruding from the center. The poles are metallic and mounted on sturdy square bases, suggesting stability for outdoor urban use. The signal lights are housed in black cylindrical casings, and the backplates are designed to enhance visibility. The two units are positioned side by side, sharing a support structure, and each has a visible mounting bracket and hardware. The overall construction appears robust, with materials likely chosen for weather resistance and durability. No pedestrian signals or control boxes are visible, and the lights are arranged horizontally.
description: A pair of yellow-backed, pole-mounted traffic lights with single black cylindrical signal lights, supported by metallic poles and square bases. The lights are horizontally arranged and built for urban outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing both traffic lights with yellow backplates and black cylindrical signal lights facing forward.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the horizontal arrangement of the two signal lights and the depth of the backplates.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the back of the yellow plates and the mounting hardware on the metallic poles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the horizontal alignment and the cylindrical shape of the signal lights.
